task_completion.description: The document meridian_correspondence.odt is opened in LibreOffice Writer.

task_completion.description: Each of the three letters begins on a new page.

task_completion.description: The subject lines are formatted as bold.

task_completion.description: The body paragraphs are formatted with justified alignment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The body paragraphs have flush (straight) left and right margins, which is characteristic of justified text alignment.

task_completion.description: The changes are saved to the document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save icon in the LibreOffice toolbar does not indicate unsaved changes (e.g., no red dot or asterisk), or a save action (File > Save) is visible in the screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice Writer GUI to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the LibreOffice Writer interface (menus, toolbar buttons) rather than terminal commands modifying the .odt file.

integrity.description: The original text content of the letters remains intact.
integrity.visual_evidence: The specific phrases, names, and identifiers (e.g., 'Riverview Commerce Center', 'Blackwood & Associates') are still present and have not been deleted or replaced with dummy text.

integrity.description: Formatting was applied using word processor features, not manual workarounds.
integrity.visual_evidence: Alignment and bolding should be applied via toolbar buttons or styles, not by manually inserting multiple spaces/tabs for alignment or typing asterisks for bolding.
